_comment: Built-in operations that have no Microsoft Learn /connectors/ page (HTTP, Request, etc.). build-firstparty.js appends these to the generated curated-first-party.json so they appear in the catalog. Keep this list small and accurate.

operations.connectorDescription: Make HTTP or HTTPS requests to any reachable endpoint. A built-in action useful for calling REST APIs and webhooks from a flow.
operations.name: HTTP
operations.description: Sends an HTTP request to the specified URL with a method, headers, and body, and returns the response.

operations.connectorDescription: Make HTTP or HTTPS requests to any reachable endpoint. A built-in action useful for calling REST APIs and webhooks from a flow.
operations.name: HTTP + Swagger
operations.description: Calls an HTTP endpoint described by an OpenAPI (Swagger) definition, with a guided parameter experience.

operations.connectorDescription: Make HTTP or HTTPS requests to any reachable endpoint. A built-in action useful for calling REST APIs and webhooks from a flow.
operations.name: HTTP Webhook
operations.description: Subscribes to and waits on an HTTP webhook callback, then continues the flow when called back.

operations.connectorDescription: Built-in triggers and responses for calling a flow over HTTP, including manual run and a When-an-HTTP-request-is-received endpoint.
operations.name: When an HTTP request is received
operations.description: Triggers the flow when an HTTP request is received at a generated endpoint URL.

operations.connectorDescription: Built-in triggers and responses for calling a flow over HTTP, including manual run and a When-an-HTTP-request-is-received endpoint.
operations.name: Response
operations.description: Returns an HTTP response with a status code, headers, and body to the caller of the flow.
